title: Summary: Turn 28
content: Pueblo is a CLI-first + Electron desktop code agent framework in TypeScript (Node.js 22), with core directories: `src/agent/` (task execution, multi-agent, Pepe semantics), `src/cli/` (terminal entry), `src/commands/` (slash commands), `src/desktop/` (Electron main/preload/renderer), `src/memory/` (embedded semantic memory with SQLite), `src/persistence/` (SQLite + migrations), `src/providers/` (LLM adapters like GitHub Copilot, DeepSeek), `src/sessions/` (session lifecycle), and `src/tools/` (exec/glob/grep/read). Key design decisions: input routing splits between slash commands and agent execution, LLM provider registry with credential store, and memory uses embedding vectors for semantic retrieval.
